# Skills Matrix Template

## Purpose
Assess team capabilities, identify skill gaps, and inform staffing, training, and risk management
decisions.

## Usage guidelines
1. Calibrate ratings before assessment.
2. Capture both current and target levels.
3. Update quarterly or after major deliveries.
4. Use results to drive training, hiring, and knowledge transfer.

## Document information
- **Project name**: [Project Name]
- **Project manager**: [Name]
- **Resource manager**: [Name]
- **Version**: [Version]
- **Last updated**: [Date]

## 1. Competency levels
| Level | Rating | Description | Characteristics |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Expert | 5 | Subject matter expert | Teaches others, leads complex initiatives |
| Advanced | 4 | Highly proficient | Works independently, mentors peers |
| Intermediate | 3 | Competent practitioner | Delivers most tasks independently |
| Basic | 2 | Foundational knowledge | Requires guidance, handles simple tasks |
| Beginner | 1 | Limited exposure | Needs training and close supervision |
| None | 0 | No experience | Requires full training |

## 2. Skills taxonomy
### 2.1 Technical skills
- Programming languages and frameworks
- Architecture and design
- Testing and automation
- DevOps and infrastructure

### 2.2 Business skills
- Domain expertise
- Process analysis
- Financial/commercial awareness
- Regulatory knowledge

### 2.3 Soft skills
- Communication and stakeholder management
- Leadership and facilitation
- Problem-solving and adaptability

### 2.4 Certifications
- Professional certifications
- Tool or platform credentials

## Completion checklist
- [ ] Ratings calibrated and agreed.
- [ ] Gap analysis completed.
- [ ] Training and transfer actions assigned.
